headline: 97°F arrives Friday — water now, blight check tomorrow.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4, c1–c4, and potato_bags today — zone 1 last ran June 14 and heat climbs from 93°F today to 97°F Friday with zero rain forecast.
Water w1, w4, and blackberry today — zone 2 also last ran June 14 and the same dry heat window applies through the weekend.
Exclude w2 and w3 garlic from all remaining irrigation — 29 days to July 15 opens the 2-week dry-down window right now.
Check c2–c4 bean seedling soil daily this week — 19-day sprouts in 93-97°F with no rain can dry out at the surface between zone 1 runs.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow on 38-day Amish Paste — inspect lower leaves now before 93–97°F heat through Friday accelerates any latent infection.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due in 5 days with harvest 29 days out — the 2-week dry-down must begin today to protect wrapper quality.

suggested_tasks.title: Train new blackberry canes to trellis
suggested_tasks.description: Five new canes observed June 14 — tie them to the trellis while growth is still flexible before Friday's 97°F hardens new wood.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w3 Italian Red for scapes
suggested_tasks.description: Hardneck Italian Red at 29 days from harvest — snap any curled scapes now to redirect energy into bulbs before the dry-down window closes.
